summary: Matched Dolphin OS.c and OSReset.c by completing missing SDK initialization/reset behavior, adding required internal prototypes and an OSThread API declaration, and flipping both objects from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The OSInit changes add startup timestamp capture, BI2/debug flag handling, alarm and memory-protection init calls, HID2 masking, arena clearing, and more accurate MetroTRK/debug handling. The OSReset changes add hot reset support, active-thread cancellation, reset-type-specific reboot/reset behavior, cache/LC and memory-clear handling, pad recalibration preservation, and an adjusted OSGetResetCode implementation.
what_changed: Marked dolphin/os/OS.c and dolphin/os/OSReset.c as Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py.
Changed EnableMetroTRKInterrupts declaration in OS.c to an explicit extern declaration.
Added global/internal OS startup state in OS.c, including __OSStartTime and BI2DebugFlagHolder.
Added ClearArena() in OS.c with boot-region-aware clearing logic using 0x812FDFF0 and 0x812FDFEC when OSGetResetCode() reports the special 0x80000000 path.

Expanded OSInit() to record __OSStartTime, handle BI2 startup/debug data from cached physical addresses, write BI2 debug flag and PAD spec bytes to 0x30E8/0x30E9, default __DVDLongFileNameFlag to 1, call OSInitAlarm(), clear HID2 bit 0x40000000, call __OSInitMemoryProtection(), and clear arena before enabling interrupts.
Added __OSInitMemoryProtection() and __OSDoHotReset(int) internal declarations to extern/dolphin/src/dolphin/os/__os.h.
Added OSReset.c helpers: __PADDisableRecalibration(int) declaration, CancelThreads(), and __OSDoHotReset(int).
Updated OSResetSystem() to distinguish reset modes: reset == 1 for hardware reset path, reset == 0 for reboot path via __OSReboot, and reset == 2 for pad recalibration disable/restore handling.

OSResetSystem() now disables LC, cancels active ready/running-like threads, zeros several low-memory regions, controls VI register state, invalidates instruction cache before reset, and restores pad recalibration state.
Changed OSGetResetCode() to return 0x80000000 when byte 0x800030E2 is nonzero, otherwise compute the reset code as (__PIRegs[9] & ~7) >> 3.
smart_moves: Converted the objects to Matching only after adding behavior-level fixes across both source and header/configuration files, rather than relying on superficial formatting changes.
Introduced a small ClearArena() helper to isolate the boot-region exception logic and keep OSInit() closer to SDK structure.
Used a local copy of next = thread->linkActive.next before OSCancelThread(thread), which avoids corrupting traversal when cancellation mutates active-thread links.
Handled reset modes explicitly with reset == 1 and reset == 0 instead of broad reset != 0 logic, which better reflects separate hot/hard reset and reboot control flow.
Added missing internal prototypes in __os.h and public thread prototype in OSThread.h so matched C can call real cross-module SDK functions without ad hoc local declarations everywhere.
Preserved and restored pad recalibration state around reset == 2 behavior, matching a subtle SDK side effect rather than just matching the obvious reset branch.
Used memory-mapped low-memory symbols/addresses directly where the SDK behavior depends on fixed boot information locations.
decomp_lessons: For Dolphin SDK OS modules, matching often requires restoring exact initialization order: time capture, interrupts, boot info, arena bounds, exception/system call/alarm/module/interrupt/SRAM/thread/audio init, HID2 update, memory protection, reports, debug interrupt enabling, arena clearing, then interrupt restore.
Reset code paths are sensitive to reset type values; replacing broad boolean reset checks with exact reset-mode comparisons can be necessary for matching and correctness.
Low-memory boot-info scratch bytes such as 0x800030E8, 0x800030E9, and 0x800030E2 can affect OS init/reset behavior and should be modeled explicitly when reconstructing Dolphin OS sources.
When a function walks intrusive OS queues and may remove/cancel nodes, save the next pointer before invoking the mutating operation.

Matching library objects may require synchronizing public SDK headers, internal headers, and build configuration together; a source-only patch can leave unresolved declarations or prevent clean matching promotion.
Special reset code 0x80000000 has downstream arena-clearing implications, so OSGetResetCode() and OSInit()/ClearArena() need to be considered together.
naming_conventions: Internal Dolphin OS functions use double-underscore prefixes, e.g. __OSInitMemoryProtection, __OSDoHotReset, __OSResetSWInterruptHandler, __OSStartTime.
Public SDK API names keep OS-prefixed PascalCase/CamelCase style, e.g. OSInit, OSResetSystem, OSGetResetCode, OSCancelThread.
Local helper functions in these SDK reconstructions use PascalCase-style names such as ClearArena and CancelThreads.
Memory-mapped boot-region macros were named in all caps as BOOT_REGION_START and BOOT_REGION_END.
Temporary or unknown fixed-address data may remain weakly named when evidence is limited, as shown by `u8 unk : 0x800030E2;` in the diff excerpt.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Kept the existing static asm Reset(unsigned long resetCode) helper and adjusted C callers to pass resetCode scaled by 8 or shifted left by 3 as needed.
Used fixed-address memory-mapped accesses and bit operations to reproduce SDK behavior, including (__PIRegs[9] & ~7) >> 3 and clearing HID2 with PPCMthid2(PPCMfhid2() & 0xBFFFFFFF).
Added dummy stack padding in OSResetSystem via `u8 _[8];`, likely to influence stack layout for matching; the diff provides no reviewer explanation, so this inference is evidence-limited.
Declared `u8 unk : 0x800030E2;` to model a byte at a fixed low-memory address used by OSGetResetCode().
Reordered and inserted OSInit calls to match the SDK's expected control-flow and side-effect sequence rather than only matching final observable state.
Used explicit casts to u32 and void* around arena and boot-info pointer arithmetic to control generated code shape.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No PR body or human text excerpt was available, so rationale is inferred from title, changed files, and diff only.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename `unk : 0x800030E2` or replace it with a documented low-memory reset flag symbol.
Check subsequent OSMemory.c work for how __OSInitMemoryProtection() was matched and declared.
Find PRs touching OSResetSW.c or OSReboot.c to see how __OSDoHotReset and OSResetSystem reset-mode behavior integrate.
Search for later cleanup around BI2DebugFlag, __DVDLongFileNameFlag, and __PADSpec low-memory handling.
Compare other Dolphin SDK OS module matching PRs for use of fixed-address declarations and stack padding tactics.
